metadata.title: EM Consulting — College Admissions Strategy
metadata.description: Wharton Huntsman Dual Degree and MIT admit. Structured admissions consulting for Chinese families targeting U.S. top universities.

hero.title: One application. One systematic plan.
hero.subtitle: Huntsman Program · MIT and Wharton dual admit. Positioning through submission, end to end.
hero.ctaSubtext: A 90-minute session to assess competitiveness and map your strategic direction

items.title: MIT + Wharton, same cycle
items.description: Not former admissions officers recalling how it worked a decade ago. Applicants who met these standards this cycle. We know the signals that matter right now because we just met them.

items.title: Five milestones, real deliverables
items.description: Written documents and acceptance checkpoints at every stage — positioning deck, profile roadmap, finalized essays, submission checklist, decision framework. Not open-ended coaching. A trackable strategy engagement.

items.title: Selective by design
items.description: We decline students we cannot meaningfully help and only take cases where we have confidence in the outcome. Fair to your family and to us.

items.title: Native bilingual communication
items.description: Mandarin-first with parents, English-first with students. No translation layer. No cultural gap.

items.title: Founding cohort · max 10
items.description: Accepting 10 families this application cycle. Even if we don't match after the diagnostic, the free 90-minute session leaves you with a candid read on your child's competitiveness.

milestones.title: Strategic Positioning
milestones.deliverable: Output: Positioning deck with core narrative direction, initial school list (8–12), and essay topic map

milestones.title: Profile Optimization
milestones.deliverable: Output: Profile roadmap covering activity adjustments, recommendation strategy, and test timeline

milestones.title: Essay Development
milestones.deliverable: Output: Finalized essays, 3–4 feedback rounds each. All content written by the student

milestones.title: Application Review
milestones.deliverable: Output: Submission checklist with full materials review, mock interviews, and parent sign-off

milestones.title: Post-Submission
milestones.deliverable: Output: Decision analysis framework covering waitlist strategy and multi-offer comparison

trackRecord.title: Founder credentials and prior mentorship
trackRecord.subtitle: EM Consulting was founded in 2026. Below are the founders' own admissions results and the schools where students Eric personally mentored (before launching the firm) were admitted.

founderCreds.name: Mary Liu
founderCreds.detail: Same cycle: MIT (EA, Astrophysics) and Wharton (ED)

trackRecord.mentorshipLabel: Schools where Eric's mentees were admitted (pre-firm)

testimonial.quote: Eric helped me through the entire process — narrowing my school list, sharpening my extracurriculars, and figuring out my personal statement. I was stuck on what story to tell, and he gave me a framework that made it click. He also reviewed all my supplementals and helped me apply to scholarships I wouldn't have found on my own. It made the whole thing a lot less stressful.
testimonial.attribution: Duke '29 · mentored by Eric before EM Consulting

trackRecord.antiObjection: Our track record is our own admissions — the same standards your child faces, met recently.

pricing.title: Transparent pricing
pricing.note: Every engagement begins with a free 90-minute diagnostic — no contract, no fee. After confirming fit, pricing is based on school count (8–12) and service depth.

audit.name: Admissions Diagnostic Report
audit.description: Standalone written report after a 90-minute session: positioning, competitiveness analysis, strategic recommendations. Purchasable on its own, no obligation to continue.

full.name: Full Package
full.description: Five-milestone engagement covering 8–12 schools. All written deliverables included, with biweekly check-ins and regular parent updates.

items.a: We aren't former admissions officers relying on decade-old experience — we're applicants who met these standards this cycle. Former AOs can spot adult-ghostwritten essays; our age proximity helps students sound authentically like their best selves. The best swimming coaches aren't Olympic swimmers — they see what the athlete can't see about their own form.

Admissions decisions are made by the schools, entirely beyond our control. We operate on the McKinsey model: fees compensate for time, expertise, and deliverables regardless of outcome. Agencies that guarantee admission are guaranteeing schools your child didn't need help getting into. We focus on maximizing probability at schools that actually matter.

items.q: What if you decline us after the diagnostic?
items.a: The 90-minute diagnostic is free regardless. If we determine fit isn't right, you still leave with a candid assessment of your child's competitiveness. For a full written roadmap, we offer a standalone Strategy Session ($500–800 USD) outside any full engagement — enough to clarify next steps on your own.

items.q: How many hours per week does the student commit?
items.a: It varies by milestone. Month 1 (Positioning): ~2–3 hours/week. Months 2–4 (Profile): 1–2 hours/week plus activity time. Months 4–7 (Essay peak): 5–8 hours/week as the student drafts and revises multiple essays. Months 7–8 (Review): 3–5 hours/week. Months 8–10 (Post-submission): 1–2 hours/week. The majority is the student's own writing and revision — we never ghostwrite, and the student is the sole author of every application material.

items.q: How are you different from legacy agencies like New Oriental or Crimson?
items.a: The U.S. admissions consulting market sits in three tiers: legacy agencies (New Oriental, etc., RMB 100–300K) — stale knowledge and hollow guarantees; boutique student-founded firms (Felix Academy, etc., RMB 50–100K) — mostly transfer consulting, not freshman admissions; freelance tutors (Xiaohongshu individuals, RMB 200–600/hr) — no structured methodology. EM Consulting is the only firm combining MIT + Wharton admissions credentials, native Mandarin communication, and a trackable 5-milestone process.

items.q: How do you prevent ghostwriting concerns on essays?
items.a: We never write or substantially rewrite any application material. Our role is brainstorming topics, providing outlines, and giving feedback on drafts the student has written — 3–4 iterative rounds per essay. We maintain written records of the revision process, and the student is the sole author of every material. This is codified in Section 2 ("Academic Integrity") of our service agreement — violations result in immediate termination without refund.

items.q: What is the refund and cancellation policy?
items.a: Full terms are in Section 5 of the Service Agreement. Summary: Before Milestone 1 (Positioning Deck) delivery — First installment (50%) refunded, minus $500 administrative fee. After Milestone 1, before Milestone 3 (Essay phase) — First installment non-refundable (core deliverables received); Second installment not yet collected. After Milestone 3 commences — Both installments non-refundable (most labor-intensive phase). Either party may terminate with 30 days written notice; pro-rata refund applies to milestones not yet commenced. The free diagnostic session carries no fee and requires no contract.
